questions.prompt: A school cafeteria packed 5 trays with 16 sandwiches on each tray. If 14 sandwiches were set aside for staff, how many sandwiches were left for students?
questions.explanation: The cafeteria packed 5 × 16 = 80 sandwiches. After 14 were set aside, 80 − 14 = 66 remained.

questions.prompt: What is the value of 4x² − 3x when x = 3?
A tank is 5/6 full. Then 1/3 of the tank is drained. What fraction of the tank remains full?
questions.explanation: Since 5/6 − 1/3 = 5/6 − 2/6 = 3/6, the tank remains 1/2 full.

questions.prompt: Which expression is equivalent to 6 − 4(2n − 3)?
A box contains 45 folders. If 18 folders are yellow, what percent of the folders are yellow?
questions.explanation: 18 out of 45 folders are yellow. Since 18/45 = 0.40, 40% are yellow.

questions.prompt: The formula A = 2(x + 5)² gives the area A of a square-based display section. If A = 98, what is one possible value of x?
questions.explanation: 98 = 2(x + 5)², so 49 = (x + 5)². One solution is x + 5 = 7, giving x = 2.

questions.prompt:
A hospital supply order includes 22 cartons. Each carton contains 15 packs, and each pack has 8 sterile pads. How many sterile pads are in the order?
Type your answer as an integer.
questions.explanation: The order contains 22 × 15 × 8 = 2,640 sterile pads.

questions.prompt: The formula P = 2a + 3b can be used to calculate points from two task types. To solve for b, first subtract [D1] from both sides, then divide by [D2].
questions.explanation: From P = 2a + 3b, subtract 2a to get P − 2a = 3b, then divide by 3.

questions.prompt: A water tank held 96 gallons at the start of the day. A hose added 3.5 gallons per minute for 12 minutes, and then 18 gallons were used. How many gallons were in the tank after these changes?
questions.explanation: The hose adds 3.5 × 12 = 42 gallons. Then 96 + 42 − 18 = 120 gallons.

questions.prompt: A value of t satisfies 4(t − 3) ≤ 28. Which statement describes all possible values of t?
questions.explanation: Divide 4(t − 3) ≤ 28 by 4 to get t − 3 ≤ 7. Add 3 to get t ≤ 10.

questions.prompt:
A table shows production data for three work teams.
Team: A, B, C
Hours worked: 5, 6, 8
Units completed: 155, 198, 240
Which team completed the most units per hour?

choices.D: Teams A and C were tied

questions.explanation: Team A: 155 ÷ 5 = 31 units per hour. Team B: 198 ÷ 6 = 33. Team C: 240 ÷ 8 = 30. Team B completed the most units per hour.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
Which expressions are equivalent to 7(3x − 2) − 4(x + 5)?
questions.explanation: Both choices correctly show the distributed form before combining like terms.

questions.prompt:
A training program recorded how participants completed a lesson.
Completion method: Laptop, Phone, Tablet, Printed packet
Participants: 38, 27, 15, 20
If one participant is selected at random, what is the probability that the participant used either a phone or a tablet?
questions.explanation: There are 100 participants total. Phone or tablet users total 27 + 15 = 42, and 42/100 simplifies to 21/50.

questions.prompt:
A formula for the total number of points earned in a course is P = 12q + 8a, where q is the number of quizzes and a is the number of assignments. If P = 252 and a = 18, what is q?
Type your answer as an integer.

A storage area is made from a rectangle with a triangular extension on one side. The rectangle is 18 feet by 11 feet. The triangle has a base of 11 feet and a height of 8 feet. What is the total area?
questions.explanation: Rectangle area is 18 × 11 = 198. Triangle area is 1/2(11)(8) = 44. Total area is 198 + 44 = 242.

questions.prompt:
The table shows part of a linear relationship.
x: −4, 2, 5, 11
y: 31, 13, 4, −14
Which equation represents the relationship?
questions.explanation: When x increases by 6, y decreases by 18, so the rate is −3. Substituting x = 2 and y = 13 gives y = −3x + 19.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A store compares two bags of dog food.
Bag: Medium, Large
Weight: 18 lb, 32 lb
Price: $28.80, $48.00
Which statements are true?

choices.A: The medium bag costs $1.60 per pound.
choices.B: The large bag costs $1.50 per pound.
choices.C: The large bag has the higher unit price.
choices.D: The medium bag costs $1.50 per pound.
choices.E: The two bags have the same unit price.

questions.correctAnswerText: The medium bag costs $1.60 per pound. | The large bag costs $1.50 per pound.
questions.explanation: Medium: 28.80 ÷ 18 = $1.60 per pound. Large: 48.00 ÷ 32 = $1.50 per pound.

questions.prompt: Which inequality represents “three times the sum of a number and 4 is at least 45”?
questions.explanation: The sum of a number and 4 is x + 4. Three times that sum is 3(x + 4), and “at least” means ≥.

questions.prompt: A store sold an item for $184 after a 15% markup from its wholesale cost. What was the wholesale cost?
questions.explanation: A 15% markup means the selling price is 115% of wholesale cost. So 184 ÷ 1.15 = 160.

questions.prompt:
Solve for x: 3(5x − 4) − 2(4x + 7) = 58.
Type your answer as an integer.
A circular tabletop has an area of about 78.5 square feet. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is the radius of the tabletop?
questions.explanation: Area = πr². So 78.5 = 3.14r², giving r² = 25 and r = 5.

questions.prompt: A line passes through the points (−8, −6) and (4, 12). What is the rate of change of the line?
A driver travels 64.8 miles in the first part of a route and 38.5 miles in the second part. The full route is 125 miles. How many miles remain?
questions.explanation: The driver has traveled 64.8 + 38.5 = 103.3 miles. The remaining distance is 125 − 103.3 = 21.7 miles.

questions.prompt:
Function A is represented by the table.
x: −2, 1, 7, 10
y: −1, 8, 26, 35
Function B is represented by y = 2.5x + 4. Which statement is true?

choices.A: Function A has a greater rate of change than Function B.
choices.B: Function B has a greater rate of change than Function A.
choices.C: Both functions have the same rate of change.

questions.correctAnswerText: Function A has a greater rate of change than Function B.
questions.explanation: Function A has rate (8 − (−1))/(1 − (−2)) = 9/3 = 3. Function B has rate 2.5, so Function A has the greater rate.

questions.prompt:
A certification score is based on 30% written work, 45% skills demonstration, and 25% attendance. A student scores 82 on written work, 90 on skills demonstration, and 96 on attendance. What is the certification score?
Type your answer as a decimal rounded to the nearest tenth.

A theater sold 138 tickets. Balcony tickets cost $18 each, and floor tickets cost $26 each. The total revenue was $3,084. Which system can be used to find b, the number of balcony tickets, and f, the number of floor tickets?
questions.explanation: The first equation represents the number of tickets, and the second represents total revenue.

questions.prompt: A rectangular image is 12 inches wide and 5 inches tall. It is enlarged by a scale factor of 1.4. What is the area of the enlarged image?
questions.explanation: Original area is 12 × 5 = 60. The enlarged area is 60 × 1.4² = 60 × 1.96 = 117.6.

questions.prompt: The equation 8x + 5y = 30 can be rewritten as y = [D1]x + [D2].
A container has 7 green beads, 5 red beads, and 8 clear beads. One bead is selected at random and not replaced. Then a second bead is selected. What is the probability that both beads are clear?
questions.explanation: There are 20 beads total and 8 are clear. Without replacement, P(two clear) = 8/20 × 7/19 = 14/95.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
The formula C = 2πr + 2s gives the perimeter C of a shape made from a semicircular edge and two straight sides of length s. Which equations correctly solve for s?
questions.explanation: From C = 2πr + 2s, subtract 2πr and divide by 2. This is equivalent to s = C/2 − πr.

questions.prompt: A metal part has a volume of 42 cubic centimeters and a density of 7.8 grams per cubic centimeter. What is its mass?
questions.explanation: Mass = density × volume = 7.8 × 42 = 327.6 grams.

questions.prompt:
A cone-shaped cup has a radius of 3 inches and a height of 7 inches. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is the volume of the cup?
Use V = 1/3πr²h.
The expression x² − 13x + 36 is equal to 0 for two values of x. Which value is one solution?
questions.explanation: x² − 13x + 36 factors as (x − 4)(x − 9), so x = 4 is one solution.

questions.prompt: A piece of equipment is modeled by V = 5,200(0.875)^t, where t is the number of years after purchase. Which statement best describes the change each year?
questions.explanation: Multiplying by 0.875 means 87.5% remains each year, so the decrease is 12.5%.

questions.prompt: A scale drawing uses 6 centimeters to represent 27 feet. A wall is 14 centimeters long on the drawing. What is the actual length of the wall?
questions.explanation: The scale is 27 ÷ 6 = 4.5 feet per centimeter. Then 14 × 4.5 = 63 feet.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

targets.T1: Subtract 4 from a number, multiply by 3, then add 8
targets.T2: Multiply a number by 3, subtract 4, then add 8
targets.T3: Add 8 to a number, multiply by 3, then subtract 4

questions.explanation: Each tile follows the order of operations described in the target.

questions.prompt: A machine prints 156 labels in 24 minutes. At this rate, how many labels will it print in 70 minutes?
questions.explanation: The rate is 156 ÷ 24 = 6.5 labels per minute. In 70 minutes, the machine prints 6.5 × 70 = 455 labels.

questions.prompt: A conference room can hold no more than 180 people. There are already 36 staff members in the room. Each invited group has 12 people. Which inequality can be used to find g, the number of invited groups that can enter?
questions.explanation: The total number of people is the 36 staff members plus 12 people for each invited group, and the room can hold no more than 180 people.

questions.prompt: A population of cells is modeled by N = 240(1.18)^t. Which statement best describes the meaning of 1.18?

choices.A: The population increases by 18% each time period.
choices.B: The population decreases by 18% each time period.
choices.D: The population increases by 1.18 cells each time period.

questions.correctAnswerText: The population increases by 18% each time period.
questions.explanation: Multiplying by 1.18 means the population keeps 100% and increases by 18%.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
The table shows the distance and time for two delivery routes.
Route: East, West
Distance: 96 mi, 135 mi
Time: 2.4 hr, 3.0 hr
Which statements are true?

choices.A: The East route averaged 40 miles per hour.
choices.B: The West route averaged 45 miles per hour.
choices.C: The West route was slower than the East route.
choices.D: The East route averaged 42 miles per hour.
choices.E: Both routes had the same average speed.

questions.correctAnswerText: The East route averaged 40 miles per hour. | The West route averaged 45 miles per hour.

questions.prompt: For the function f(x) = −2x² + 5x + 12, what is f(−2)?

The table shows a relationship.
x: −1, 0, 1, 2
y: 9, 4, 1, 0
Which statement best describes the relationship?

choices.A: It is linear because the x-values increase by 1.
choices.B: It is linear because the y-values decrease.
choices.C: It is not linear because the changes in y are not constant.
choices.D: It is not linear because one y-value is 0.

questions.correctAnswerText: It is not linear because the changes in y are not constant.
questions.explanation: The changes in y are −5, −3, and −1, so the relationship is not linear.

questions.prompt: Which expression represents 9 more than one fourth of the difference of a number x and 12?
questions.explanation: The difference of x and 12 is x − 12. One fourth of that difference is 1/4(x − 12), and 9 more is 1/4(x − 12) + 9.

questions.prompt:
The formula A = 1/2(a + b)h gives the area of a trapezoid. If A = 168, a = 14, and h = 8, what is b?
Type your answer as an integer.
A rectangular parking area is 54 feet long and 36 feet wide. A rectangular storage shed that is 12 feet by 9 feet is placed in one corner. What area of the parking area remains uncovered?
questions.explanation: Parking area is 54 × 36 = 1,944 square feet. Shed area is 12 × 9 = 108 square feet. Uncovered area is 1,944 − 108 = 1,836 square feet.
